title: strychnos canthioides
intro: Strychnos canthioides, also known as the African strychnine tree or Strychnos, is a plant species belonging to the Loganiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Strychnos canthioides include its potential to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for snake bites and other poisonous bites. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds, leaves, and bark are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
